title: Urban Bees
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Urban beekeeping has (0) become increasingly popular in recent years as cities embrace the idea of keeping honeybees on rooftops, in parks, and even in community gardens. This trend reflects growing awareness about the importance of bees for pollination and food security.||Surprisingly, cities can actually provide excellent habitats (9) for bees. Urban areas often have diverse flowering plants throughout the growing season, from gardens and parks (10) to roadside plantings. Cities also tend to use fewer pesticides than agricultural areas, (11) which can be harmful to bee populations. Urban bees have been found to produce high-quality honey, sometimes (12) with unique flavours reflecting the variety of flowers in their environment.||Many cities now encourage urban beekeeping (13) by providing training programmes and relaxing regulations that previously restricted the practice. Organisations teach aspiring beekeepers about bee biology, hive management, and safety. Some hotels and businesses have installed rooftop hives (14) as part of sustainability initiatives, while schools use beekeeping to teach students about ecology and food systems.||However, urban beekeeping must (15) be done responsibly. Too many hives in a small area can lead to competition for resources, potentially harming wild bee populations. Urban beekeepers need to ensure their colonies are healthy and don't spread diseases. (16) When properly managed, urban beehives can contribute to urban biodiversity, provide educational opportunities, and help people reconnect with nature in city environments.

example.text: Urban beekeeping has (0) increasingly popular in recent years.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'habitats for bees' indica qué especies habitan allí.
Expresión 'from...to' indica rango de ubicaciones con flores.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere a los pesticidas.
Preposición 'with' introduce características adicionales de la miel.
Preposición 'by' introduce el método para fomentar la apicultura.
Preposición 'as' indica el rol: 'as part of initiatives'.
Voz pasiva modal: 'must be done' expresa obligación.
Conjunción temporal que introduce la condición para la contribución.
